source_title: SpeedBridge Knotless Rotator Cuff Repair (technique guide)
text: the FiberTape suture limbs individually. Apply tension to the FiberTape sutures so that the tissue is reduced and compressed to the bone. Cut the FiberTape suture tails with a FiberTape cutter. Repeat these steps for the second lateral anchor. Completely advance the driver into the bone socket, beyond the first laser line, until the anchor body contacts bone. Evaluate suture tension. If tension is not adequate, back the driver out and readjust the tension. Note: Do not attempt to apply tension to the sutures with the eyelet in the bone socket. Make sure the tip of the anchor body is in contact with bone. Hold the thumb pad steady and rotate the driver handle clockwise to insert the anchor body until it is flush with the bone. SpeedBridge™ Technique: Lateral Row Fixation 1 53 4 2 SpeedBridge™ Technique: Lateral Row SP Fixation SpeedBridge™ Technique: Lateral Row SP Fixation I 09 Retrieve one FiberTape® suture limb from each medial anchor and load them through the self-punching SwiveLock anchor eyelet using the megaloader. Completely advance the driver into the bone socket, beyond the first laser line, until the anchor body contacts bone. Evaluate suture tension. If tension is not adequate, back the driver out and readjust tension. Note: Do not attempt to apply tension to the sutures with the eyelet in the bone socket. Make sure the tip of the anchor body is in contact with bone. Hold the thumb pad steady and rotate the driver handle in a clockwise direction to insert the anchor body until it is flush with the bone. Cut the FiberTape suture tails with a FiberTape cutter. Repeat steps for the second lateral anchor. With the eyelet of the implant above bone, remove slack and tension each suture to reduce the tissue and compress it to bone. Note: If hard bone is encountered, use a punch to break the cortex before inserting the anchor. Be sure the eyelet and driver are perpendicular to the bone prior to malleting. SpeedBridge™ Technique: Lateral Row SP Fixation 1 53 4 2 SpeedBridge™ Technique: Knotless Double-Pulley SpeedBridge™ Technique: Knotless Double-Pulley I 11 Insert the Knotless SwiveLock® anchor into the medial row and pass the FiberTape® suture. Load the single end of a FiberLink™ suture onto a Scorpion™ suture passer and pass inside the FiberTape sutures. (a) Blue Repair Stitch (b) White/Black Shuttle Stitch • Flat SutureTape (pull end) • Round Suture Loop (loading end) (c) Two White/Black TigerTape™ sutures spliced into one #2 TigerWire® suture (d) White/Black Repair Stitch (e) White/Blue Shuttle Stitch • Flat SutureTape (pull end) • Round Suture Loop (loading end) (f) Two Blue/White FiberTape sutures spliced into one #2 FiberWire® suture Retrieve the single end of the FiberLink suture from a superior portal to improve the shuttling angle through the tissue. Retrieve the Knotless SwiveLock sutures and load them into the loop of the FiberLink suture. Pull the FiberLink suture to shuttle and pass the sutures through the tissue. Repeat for the other medial row anchor. Knotless SwiveLock Suture Layout Tendon reduction under direct visualization and tensionable knotless medial fixation can be achieved with the knotless double-pulley. The tensionable knotless sutures are shuttled through the cuff separately from the FiberTape sutures to help prevent twists in the construct on the underside of the rotator cuff. SpeedBridge™ Technique: Knotless Double-Pulley 21 a b c d e f I SpeedBridge™ Technique: Knotless Double-Pulley 12 Retrieve the posterior blue repair suture (a) and the anterior looped end of the white/blue shuttle suture (e) from the lateral cannula. Feed the end of the repair suture through the loop of the shuttle suture and fold at the ink- mark indicator. Pull the tape suture tail of the white/blue shuttle suture (e) to shuttle the repair suture into the knotless mechanism. Repeat the previous steps using the anterior white/black repair suture and the posterior looped end of the white/black shuttle suture. 3 4 Tension the two repair sutures, reducing and fixating the tendon to the bone. Tension the FiberTape® suture limbs independently to remove any potential slack from under the tendon. Cut the repair sutures flush once adequate fixation is achieved. Refer back to Lateral Row Fixation to complete the repair. 5 SpeedBridge™ Technique: Dog-Ear Fixation SpeedBridge™ Technique: Dog-Ear Fixation I 15 Achieve anterior and posterior lateral tissue fixation using the knotless repair stitches from Knotless SwiveLock® anchors. SpeedBridge™ Technique: Dog-Ear Fixation Retrieve the knotless sutures from the lateral cannula. Load the blue repair suture onto a Scorpion™ suture passer and pass through tissue. Tension the repair suture, reducing and fixating the tendon to the bone. Cut the repair suture flush once adequate fixation is achieved.
